skill.name: deep-research
skill.description: Conduct structured deep research with multi-source retrieval, citation verification, report generation, and quality checks for complex questions.
skill.summary: Build citation-backed research reports from many sources with verification and synthesis.
skill.author: lumacoder

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many critical and high patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown examples, templates, CSS names, and research terminology. Confirmed risks are legitimate for this skill: web citation checks, local report and state-file writes, generated HTML, and autonomous validation commands. No evidence found of malicious intent, credential exfiltration, persistence, or prompt injection aimed at bypassing the audit.

medium_findings.title: Autonomous Filesystem Writes to User Directories
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to create report folders in Documents and duplicate output under a hidden Claude directory. Helper scripts also write research state and bibliography files. This is expected for report generation, but users should understand that local files are created and updated.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is direct evidence of file creation and writes in user-controlled or home-directory paths. The behavior is aligned with the skill purpose, so it is a moderate operational risk rather than malicious activity.

medium_findings.title: Network Requests for Citation Verification
medium_findings.description: The citation verifier contacts doi.org and checks report URLs with HTTP HEAD requests. This is legitimate verification behavior, but it sends selected citation identifiers and URLs to external services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code constructs external requests and opens URLs with a timeout. No credential exfiltration is evident, but external network access is confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Generated HTML May Include Unescaped Report Content
medium_findings.description: The Markdown-to-HTML converter performs regular expression substitutions and paragraph conversion without an explicit HTML escaping step in the reviewed range. If untrusted source text is copied into a report, generated HTML that is opened in a browser could render unsafe markup.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The converter clearly transforms text into HTML and the skill instructs opening the result. I did not confirm a concrete exploit path, so this remains a moderate review item.

medium_findings.title: Autonomous External Command and Agent Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill asks agents to run local Python validation scripts and spawn continuation agents for long reports. This supports the intended workflow, but it increases execution scope and should be disclosed to users before marketplace publication.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and agent orchestration are explicit in the skill instructions. The commands are fixed validation/conversion steps, so the risk is elevated but not evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Markdown and Template False Positives
low_findings.description: Many weak crypto, C2, reconnaissance, and external command hits are Markdown examples, table placeholders, CSS class names, or documentation text. I did not find evidence that these matches perform malicious execution or command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation examples, report placeholders, or CSS selectors. They do not show executable malware logic or credential exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Search Found Only Skill Workflow Instructions
low_findings.description: I found headings such as Core System Instructions and CRITICAL INSTRUCTIONS, but they describe the skill workflow and continuation prompt. No evidence found of audit bypass language, pre-approval claims, or instructions to ignore marketplace security review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious phrases exist, but their context is ordinary skill operation. I did not find override language aimed at the evaluator or marketplace audit.

low_findings.title: Non-Security Hashing Used for Citation Identifiers
low_findings.description: The citation manager imports hashing and generates short citation IDs from URLs. This is not used for passwords, signatures, integrity guarantees, or cryptographic authentication.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hash is used to derive a compact citation key from a URL. Weak cryptographic properties are not relevant to this non-security identifier use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Write Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts write state and bibliography data to paths supplied by the workflow. This should remain limited to report output locations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Direct file write calls are present. The surrounding code indicates report persistence rather than destructive behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: External URL Request Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The citation verifier reaches external DOI and source URLs to validate bibliography entries.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code uses urllib request calls to external URLs. The intent is citation validation and no secret transmission was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Regex-Based HTML Generation Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The converter builds HTML with substitutions over Markdown content. This is a review point when report text includes untrusted excerpts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The transformation is visible, but the full report generation path may avoid raw untrusted HTML. Manual review should confirm escaping before publication.

content.value_statement: Complex research questions require more than a few searches and scattered notes. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through structured retrieval, verification, synthesis, and reporting.
actual_capabilities: Guides an eight-phase research workflow from scoping through packaged reporting.
Defines quick, standard, deep, and ultradeep modes for different time budgets.
Requires multiple sources and citation-backed claims in final reports.
Includes scripts for report validation, citation checks, and Markdown-to-HTML conversion.
Provides templates for structured Markdown and professional HTML research reports.
Supports continuation workflows for long reports assembled in sections.
limitations: It depends on available web access and source quality for current information.
Citation checks can fail when publishers block DOI or URL verification requests.
Generated reports still require human review for critical business or legal decisions.
The workflow creates local report files and may use hidden Claude output folders.

use_cases.title: Evaluate a Strategic Market
use_cases.description: Produce a structured report that compares market signals, competitors, risks, and evidence quality.

use_cases.title: Review Technical Options
use_cases.description: Compare frameworks, architectures, or implementation approaches with cited tradeoffs and recommendations.

use_cases.title: Prepare Evidence Briefs
use_cases.description: Synthesize academic, industry, and regulatory sources into a clear brief with limitations and citations.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Research Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a clear topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use deep-research in standard mode to create a cited research brief on [topic]. Include key findings, limitations, and recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Decision support for competing tools or strategies.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use deep-research to compare [option A] and [option B] for [context]. Evaluate evidence, tradeoffs, risks, and decision criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate a Current Trend
prompt_templates.prompt: Use deep-research to analyze [trend] over the last [time period]. Prioritize primary sources, recent data, and conflicting viewpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Executive Decision Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced research with high rigor requirements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use deep mode to produce an executive report on [decision]. Include source quality scoring, counterevidence, assumptions, and a recommendation.

output_examples.input: Research whether our team should adopt a new observability platform.
output_examples.output: A report with selection criteria, vendor comparison, implementation risks, cited evidence, and a final recommendation.

output_examples.input: Analyze the 2026 state of AI coding assistants for enterprise development.
output_examples.output: An evidence-backed trend brief covering capabilities, adoption barriers, security concerns, and near-term outlook.

output_examples.input: Compare React, Vue, and Svelte for a regulated internal dashboard.
output_examples.output: A decision matrix with maintainability, ecosystem, accessibility, security, and hiring considerations.

best_practices: State the research question, audience, geography, and time window before starting.
Ask for source quality, counterevidence, and uncertainty notes in the final report.
Review generated citations and files before using the report for important decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for simple facts that need one direct lookup.
Do not treat generated citations as final without verification.
Do not request private, confidential, or regulated data unless local handling is approved.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces structured research reports with source-backed findings, synthesis, limitations, recommendations, and bibliography sections.

faq.question: Does it browse the web?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow expects web search and URL or DOI checks when those tools are available.

faq.question: Can it create files?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to save Markdown, HTML, PDF, state, and validation outputs locally.

faq.question: Is it suitable for quick questions?
faq.answer: No. The skill says simple lookups should use normal search instead of the full research pipeline.

faq.question: Does it replace expert review?
faq.answer: No. It improves research structure, but experts should review important findings, citations, and recommendations.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
